blockingIssues.description: `engine/src/engine.c` still loses repetition history in the documented frontend flow because `set_position()` only preserves history for identical FENs or one-ply successors, while `search_best_move()` does not advance `engine_position` after returning the engine move. The next frontend `set_position(fen)` therefore arrives two plies later and resets repetition tracking, so threefold detection remains broken across engine turns.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/architecture.md` to reflect that the worker now boots the engine with `importScripts("/engine/engine.js")` and exposes the `searchBestMove(fen, depth, timeMs)` hook signature.
Updated `.factory/library/engine-book-api.md` to document the embedded `/book.bin` WASM book path and the still-incomplete repetition-history behavior in the frontend `set_position(fen)` flow.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document both supported worker-loading strategies for the Emscripten bundle: ES-module `import()` with `-s EXPORT_ES6=1`, and classic-worker `importScripts()` with `createChessEngine` on the worker global scope.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The fix feature intentionally switched to the classic-worker path and recorded that deviation, but `.factory/skills/integration-worker/SKILL.md` still only describes the ES-module import flow.
